mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation remains below 3 percent.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening labor demand and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 265, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses above 265, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes after 2005.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized macroeconomic demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The negative signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.5 percent.
